© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Key Takeaways:
• We’re now going to talk about the actual sequence of your open cart. First of all,
you should leave your cart open for 3 to 7 days
• For most people 5 to 7 is golden.
• More than 7 days is a beg-a-thon
• You can do shorter but this is an advanced technique. In general, it’ll you and your
partners less time to adjust as things happen during the launch
• My favorite opening days are Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday. Friday also works.
This does depend on the market though - they all react differently
• For closing days, just avoid the weekends. Make sure to close at midnight and not
during the middle of the day
• Days of the week I’ve used:
o Open Monday, close Friday (5 days)
o Open Tuesday, close Thursday (3 days)
o Open Wednesday, close Friday (3 days)
o Open Tuesday, close Monday (7 days)
o Open Thursday, close Monday (5 days)
o Open Thursday, close Wednesday (7 days)
• Don’t overanalyze this - it’s not rocket science and it’s flexible
• When to Send Emails:
o Cart open day: two emails
o First email: “We’re open” - very short email, make sure to keep this brief
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 7 --

3
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
o Second email: “Tsunami email” - something quirky happens usually during
launch so use this as an opportunity to email people again
o Send an email EVERY DAY during your launch
• Answering objections:
o What’s happening with people who’ve bought
o Feedback you’ve gotten
• The day before you close cart you must create scarcity - many ways to do this:
o Close cart
o Price goes up
o Bonuses go away
o Cart closing day: Send two or three e-mails
o 6 AM: we're closing tonight
o 12 PM: We're closing in 12 hours
o 6 PM: Last chance - “thank you”
o This is important - you need to close down with power or else you will
leave a lot of sales on the table

-- 3 of 7 --

4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
I want to talk about your actual sequence of your Open Cart. This is more about
e-mails than how you’re messaging your list.
You want to leave your cart open anywhere from three to seven days. I think for
most people five to seven days is that golden period. If you go longer than seven days, in
my opinion, it ends up turning into a beg-a-thon because you're going to want to keep up
the momentum and keep up the pressure in continuing to e-mail throughout your Open
Cart. Even at seven days usually at the end of it it feels like you're running on fumes, you
been sending the same type of e-mails over and over so that's why I like to do it five to
seven days. You could do shorter and I think it's more of an advance technique to go
shorter. A lot of times I have gone a day and a half, two days, maybe three days for my
launches but I've done a lot of them.
The thing is with the shorter launches it gives you less time to adjust if things are
not going the way you want them to go. That Sales Video just really isn't all that good, it's
not converting as well as I want so I'm going to re-shoot the Sales Video. Well if you've
only got the cart open for two or three days it's hard to do something like that. If you
have it open five to seven days you can adjust. Some partners that didn't get their e-mail
out for some reason, that's fine, they can adjust. So shorter period launches they really
build scarcity and position in moving forward in the market but things can go wrong. I
think five to seven days is that golden period.
Then favor opening days are Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday. Friday also works.
I've actually had a lot of Friday opens and they've worked just fine. You generally don't
want to open on the weekends. Now this is market dependent; there are some market
where it's better to open on the weekends. I've heard the dating advice market works
really great on Saturday because all these guys are home on Saturdays wishing they had a
date. So different markets can react differently but across most markets, Tuesday,
Wednesday, Thursday, specifically Tuesday and Thursday are good days to open up.
Favorite closing days in general for most markets you want to avoid the
weekends. If you are going to close on the weekend then don't do it on Saturday, close on
Sunday. Also you want to close at midnight. You don't want to close in the middle of the
day. Close at midnight otherwise you're leaving money on the table.
Here are some of the ways I have structured it and there's a lot of different ways,
lot of flexibility. Open Monday close Friday, that's a five-day launch. I've done that
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 7 --

5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
recently; it's worked out pretty well. Open Tuesday close Thursday, that's a short one, a
three-day launch. I've opened Wednesday and closed Friday. That's a three-day launch as
well. It's just moved one day there so you see how flexible you can be. I've opened up on
Tuesday and closed on the following Monday. That's a seven-day launch that bridged
across a weekend. I've opened on Thursday and closed on Monday, that's a five-day
launch that bridged a weekend. I've opened on Thursday and closed on Wednesday, a
seven-day launch that bridged the weekend.
Obviously you can move these around. All these are five or seven or three. Can
you do six or four? Absolutely. You can open on Thursday and close on Tuesday, that's a
six-day launch. The reality is most people are on mine almost every day. In general e-
mails get opened more on Tuesday and Wednesdays and Thursdays than they do on
Mondays or Saturdays. In general, in most markets. But you can test this out. If you're
just watching your e-mails and watching when the open rates tend to be, you'll see what
are the good days in your market. But this isn't rocket science and it isn't science
whatsoever, there is a lot of flexibility. Don't worry about which days, don't worry about
that, it's not important, just get the open right and you'll be fine.
Now, when do you send e-mails during a launch, or during your Open Cart? And
the answer is you generally send them a lot more than people think you send them. So on
your cart opening day, you want to send two e-mails. Actually I usually send three e-
mails. I send an e-mail before the cart opens giving people the final instructions, telling
them what to expect once the cart opens, and then you send the old, "We're open, go buy
it, click here, go check it out, you been looking forward to it." So that's a very short e-
mail - 15 words, "Hey we're open, click here." It can be very, very short e- mail. Hopefully
they're anticipating that.
Then maybe three, four, five hours after you Open Cart you want to send the
tsunami e-mail. This is something I've been teaching since the first version of PLF
probably around 2005. The tsunami e-mail is this e-mail where you're giving the
impression, and this is often the case, that you're just trying to keep your head above
water. That this whole thing has become this epic event that's carrying, threatening to
carry you away. The feeling in this e-mail is we're all in this together, hang in there, I'm
just trying to stay one step ahead of you and keep the server up, and make sure
everything runs well.
On opening cart day there's often some quirky thing happens that you don't
expect that throws a curve ball at you. Use that as a reason to e-mail people and say,
"Hey, you might have had some trouble getting through the site," or "We had this link
that wasn't working, thanks for hanging in there. The good news is we've got everything
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 7 --

6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
fixed, and orders going through OK, lots of people have already jumped on board." If it's
a digital product, "Some people are already in the membership site and they're building a
great community." That's the second e-mail on opening day.
Then you want to e-mail every single day during your launch. So if you have a
seven-day Open Cart, that means you send those two e-mails on day one. Then day two,
three, four, five, and six you're sending an additional e-mail every single day.
Often I'm sending an FAQ e-mail, I might send a little bonus video, but you just
keep on coming up with reasons to mail them. That's why I say, once you get that launch
longer than seven days, it's hard to keep on coming up with reasons to keep on mailing
them. You can send e-mails talking about some of the objections and answering some of
the objections. Talk about what's happening with the people that actually bought your
product, You've actually got it in their hands now, the results you're getting, or the
feedback you're getting from them.
Then the day before you close cart, you want to start talking about the scarcity.
When I say close cart, not everyone is going to close their shopping cart and take their
product off the market. Lot of people do with their launches but there always has to be
some definitive end to your launch. You had an open and then you have a close for the
launch. The product might stay on the market but something bad happens if they don't
buy. You create a deadline at the end of your launch.
Generally, there's three ways to create scarcity. First is that bonuses go away so
the offer gets less attractive. You put together this bonus package for the launch and the
bonuses go away. The second is the price goes up. There's this [inaudible 08:30] launch
special, you can get it for $97, after the launch it's going to be $197. So that's reason
number two or scarcity reason number two. The third one is the project comes off the
market, you're actually going to close the cart and they aren't going to be able to get it.
Those three, I named them in that order, that's the order of power. From the least
powerful to the most powerful. The least powerful is the bonuses go away. Still powerful,
still good, scarcity, but that's less powerful than the price is going up. Price is going up is
strong but it's not as strong as the projects actually coming off the market.
Actually you can combine all three. You can say hey, "We're going to close the cart
and after we close the cart, we might in the future, bring this back on the market. When
we do, if we do, all these bonuses, Bonus A, B, and C that I told you about, we're not
going to offer those ever again. And when we bring it back the price is going to go up to
X. You got to act by tonight or else it's coming off the market and if we ever release it
again, you're not going to be able to get these bonuses, and the price will go up to X.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 7 --

7
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
That's combining all three.
But any way, the day before you close cart you start messaging scarcity. If you're
going to close on a Friday, you open on Monday you send two e-mails on Monday, you
send another e-mail on Tuesday, one on Wednesday, one on Thursday. You might even
send two on Thursday. Send one early in the morning, that might be an FAQ, or a little
bit of content, or answering objection. But then on midday on Thursday, you would send
something that says hey, "We're going to be closing down tomorrow. If you want to jump
on board you got to act now, we're closing tomorrow at midnight." And you do want to
close at midnight. I think I mentioned that already.
You don't want to close in the middle of the day. So you close at midnight.
So the day before cart close, you want to start messaging scarcity, possibly send
two e-mails on that day. Then on closing day you want to send two or three e-mails.
Lot of people are like, "Jeff I can't send two e-mails. I've never sent two e-mails
on the same day before." The thing is you don't have to send two e-mails if you want.
The only thing is you're going to leave half your sales on the table. Because half of your
sales likely will come in on your last day. That's why it's important to mail multiple times.
Three e-mails, how do you actually send three e-mails to your list? Well at six am.
You send an e-mail says you're closing tonight at midnight. Midnight whatever Pacific
time, Eastern time, whatever you want, we're closing tonight at midnight. Then in the
middle of the day you send another e-mail that says we're going to close in 12 hours.
Then at six pm. You send them, "This is your last chance." Often I'll send a thank you.
This last one will be, "Hey I just wanted to . . . Wow this has been a crazy
experience, I've put out all these videos, got all these comments, it's just been a
wonderful experience to go through, thanks for sharing it with me. Thank you for
everyone whose already jumped on board with me. We've already got tons of people in
the membership site and they're all creating this great community together. Oh by the
way, if you haven't jumped on board you still have a few hours to go and we're closing
tonight at midnight." That's how you do three e-mails on the final day.
You definitely follow this. At times people pull a punch. You got to go for the
jugular, you have to close this thing down with power and this is exactly how you do it.
Send two e-mails on that first day, send an additional e-mail every day your open, the
second to last day start sending scarcity e-mail, consider sending two e-mails on that day.
Then the final day send three e-mails.
